items.title: Accessible abortion services
items.desc: Warm and cosy -- it is not only an abortion clinic, but a haven for women and LGBTIs to reclaim power over their bodies.

items.title: Small firms under pressure as costs soar
items.desc: Thai small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) face a wall of negative sentiment this year as geopolitical tensions, trade conflicts and tighter access to loans weigh on the sector, raising questions about how businesses can navigate these tumultuous times.

items.title: Cautious developers to cut Bangkok housing launches
items.desc: New residential supply launched in Greater Bangkok this year is expected to continue to decline after hitting a multi-year low in 2025, as a large amount of inventory remains unsold and developers become more cautious about investment.

items.title: War impact likely to unfold in phases
items.desc: Thailand's economy and equity market could face mounting pressure if tensions between the US-Israel alliance and Iran escalate, with risks evolving from short-term financial market volatility to a deeper economic slowdown, says InnovestX Securities.

items.title: Economic toll of war grows
items.desc: The escalating war between the US-Israel alliance and Iran is entering a more dangerous phase, raising concerns the conflict could trigger a wider economic shock via surging energy prices, disrupted trade routes and weakening global demand.

items.title: Revitalised city park gets rave reviews
items.desc: Bangkok's Lumpini Park -- long regarded as the city's green heart -- is undergoing a quiet but significant transformation.

items.title: Govt in talks for more oil
items.desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul says Thailand is negotiating with all major oil-producing nations, not only Russia, to secure energy supplies, adding the nation will not face an energy shortage.

items.title: Tourist seriously hurt after falling from hotel building in Pattaya
items.desc: Rescue workers from Sawang Boriboon assist a foreign woman who was seriously injured after falling from a hotel building in Pattaya before rushing her to hospital for treatment. PATTAYA, Thailand – A foreign woman was seriously injured after falling from a hotel building in Pattaya during the early hours of March 15. At approximately 4:06 a.m., the rescue center of Sawang Boriboon received a report of a person falling from a height at a hotel located in Soi Bongkot 8 in South Pattaya. Rescue volunteers, together with officers from Pattaya City Police Station, rushed to the scene to investigate

items.title: Shorter booking windows force Pattaya tourism to rethink promotions
items.desc: Sanphet Suphaborwornsathien, Assistant Managing Director of The Zign Group, says tourism operators in Pattaya are closely monitoring global developments as rising fuel costs and flight disruptions linked to tensions in the Middle East begin to affect travel patterns. PATTAYA, Thailand – Rising tensions in the Middle East are beginning to ripple through the global travel industry, with tourism operators in Pattaya closely monitoring the situation as fuel prices climb and some international flights face disruptions. The conflict has driven oil prices higher worldwide, increasing fuel costs for a

items.title: Pattaya officers wake intoxicated tourist found sleeping on bank steps
items.desc: Municipal officers in Pattaya assist an intoxicated foreign tourist found sleeping on the steps of Bangkok Bank along Pattaya Second Road before helping ensure his safety. PATTAYA, Thailand – Municipal enforcement officers in Pattaya assisted a heavily intoxicated foreign tourist who was found asleep on the steps of Bangkok Bank along Pattaya Second Road on March 15. Officers checked on the tourist after noticing him lying on the staircase, a situation that posed potential risks to both his safety and belongings. Authorities woke the man and asked about his accommodation before helping escort

items.title: Public pressure mounts as Pattaya reviews Bongkot–Kor Phai traffic barrier
items.desc: Traffic builds up near the Bongkot–Kor Phai junction in South Pattaya as city officials consider reopening a road barrier following public complaints that the closure has worsened congestion in the area. PATTAYA, Thailand – City officials in Pattaya are preparing to reopen a traffic barrier at the intersection of Soi Bongkot and Soi Kor Phai in South Pattaya after numerous complaints from residents and motorists about worsening congestion. The proposal is currently being discussed with relevant agencies after local drivers urged authorities to reconsider the barrier, which had been installed e

items.title: Pattaya officials confiscate illegal beach chairs and mats on public sand
items.desc: Municipal officers in Pattaya remove mats and chairs illegally placed for rent on the sand at Pattaya Beach, warning vendors that public beach space cannot be occupied for private business without permission. PATTAYA, Thailand – Authorities in Pattaya have announced stricter enforcement against individuals illegally placing mats and chairs on public beaches for rental, warning that violators will face immediate confiscation of equipment and fines. City officials said the move comes after complaints that some operators have been setting up mats and folding chairs along the beachfront and rentin

items.title: Pattaya Wan Lai Set for April 19 as Chonburi Celebrates Extended Songkran Festivities
items.desc: Crowds celebrate the Wan Lai water festival along Pattaya Beach in Pattaya, where the city will host its famous Songkran finale on April 19 as part of the extended celebrations across Chonburi Province. PATTAYA, Thailand – One of Thailand’s most famous Songkran celebrations is set to return to Pattaya on April 19 with the annual Wan Lai festival, the grand finale of Chonburi’s extended water celebrations that stretch across the province for nearly the entire month. While Songkran is officially celebrated nationwide from April 13–15, Chonburi province continues the festive spirit with a series

items.title: Hot days and possible thunderstorms forecast as Pattaya and Eastern Thailand see changing weather
items.desc: Foreign tourists relax on the sand at Pattaya Beach in Chonburi Province as clouds gather along the coastline, with the Thai Meteorological Department forecasting hot daytime conditions and a chance of scattered thunderstorms across eastern Thailand. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – Thailand’s Meteorological Department has forecast hot daytime conditions across much of the country, with isolated thunderstorms expected in several regions, including eastern provinces where the coastal city of Pattaya is located. items.title: Gold robbery suspect arrested as Thai police vow to protect public safety and tourism
items.desc: Pol. Gen. Thatchai Pitaneelaboot, Deputy Commissioner-General of the Royal Thai Police, speaks about the swift arrest of a suspect in a Nakhon Pathom gold shop robbery and warns that police will take firm action against anyone attempting to disrupt public safety in Thailand, including major tourist destinations such as Pattaya. PATTAYA, Thailand – Thai police have arrested a suspect in connection with a gold shop robbery at a shopping mall in Nakhon Pathom, reaffirming that criminals who disrupt public safety will be tracked down and brought to justice. items.title: Egg prices rise across Thailand but Pattaya restaurants and hotels say supply still stable
items.desc: Egg trays are displayed for sale at a market as Thailand’s egg producers announce a price increase starting March 16, while suppliers say tourist destinations such as Pattaya continue to have sufficient eggs to meet demand from residents and visitors. PATTAYA, Thailand – Thailand’s egg producer network has announced a recommended increase in farm-gate egg prices, raising the cost of mixed eggs from 3.20 baht to 3.40 baht per egg, or about 6 baht more per tray. The adjustment will take effect on March 16. Producers say the increase follows a prolonged period of low prices that forced farmers to

items.title: Panic fuel buying hits parts of Thailand but Pattaya stations remain calm for now
items.desc: A petrol station displays “fuel finished” and purchase limit signs outside its forecourt as motorists rush to fill their tanks amid fears of price increases, while authorities say the shortages are caused by panic buying and insist national fuel supplies remain sufficient. PATTAYA, Thailand – Reports of motorists rushing to fill their tanks have led to temporary fuel shortages and purchase limits at some petrol stations across parts of Thailand, while authorities insist national fuel supplies remain stable and urge the public not to panic or hoard fuel. Local media reported that several statio

items.title: Plenty of fuel, govt insists, as motorists drain pumps dry
items.desc: Government officials have rushed to reassure motorists that "out of stock" signs reported at some petrol stations were caused by private motorists and public transport operators panicked into topping up by the Middle East war, not by a shortage of fuel reserves in Thailand.

items.title: People’s Party’s access to ID verification system blocked after data breach
items.desc: The Department of Public Administration has blocked the People’s Party (PP) from using its digital ID verification system, after the party's membership database was hacked.

items.title: Bhumjaithai’s Sophon elected House Speaker
items.desc: The House of Representatives on Sunday elected Bhumjaithai’s Sophon Zaram as speaker, with 289 votes, defeating People’s Party list MP Parit Wacharasindhu, who received 123.

items.title: Border petrol stations in Mae Sot suspend services as fuel runs out
items.desc: Border petrol stations in Mae Sot suspend services as fuel runs out Legacy Several petrol stations along the Thai-Myanmar border in Mae Sot district, Tak province, were forced to temporarily suspend services today, March 15, after running out of fuel amid a rise in price and demand. At around 5.30pm, large numbers of vehicles, including cargo trucks, agricultural transport vehicles, private cars and motorcycles, were queuing for fuel along Asian Highway 12, where many petrol stations are located. The long lines caused traffic congestion in several areas, prompting police to step in to manage t

items.title: Israeli man arrested in Krabi cannabis extract factory raid
items.desc: Israeli man arrested in Krabi cannabis extract factory raid Legacy Police in Krabi arrested an Israeli man yesterday, March 14, after raiding a facility suspected of illegally producing and selling cannabis extracts in what officials described as a factory-style operation. The raid took place at a business in Sai Thai subdistrict, Mueang Krabi district, after investigators obtained a search warrant from Krabi Provincial Court. Officers from multiple agencies joined the operation after receiving intelligence that cannabis extracts were being produced and distributed illegally. Photo via อสมท ภู

items.title: Two men nabbed for porcupine poaching in Nakhon Si Thammarat
items.desc: Two men nabbed for porcupine poaching in Nakhon Si Thammarat Legacy Two men caught hunting protected wildlife were arrested early this morning, March 15, after authorities found them with porcupine carcasses and a firearm in a protected forest area in Nakhon Si Thammarat. Officials from Nam Tok Yong National Park, the Regional Office of Protected Areas 5, and the Phaya Suea task force carried out the operation at around 2.45am in a forest area overlapping Khao Luang National Reserved Forest and Namtok Yong National Park in Lan Saka district. Officers said the operation followed a tip-off from

items.title: Thailand says fuel reserves sufficient amid global oil concerns
items.desc: Thailand says fuel reserves sufficient amid global oil concerns Legacy Thailand is unlikely to face a fuel shortage despite global uncertainties, Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul said today, March 15, urging the public to conserve energy while reassuring that oil supplies remain stable . Speaking at the House of Parliament, Anutin said Thailand would maintain oil sales to Laos, adding that the country is not facing any immediate fuel shortages. He said major oil traders, including Thai Oil, Bangchak, Shell, and SPRC (Caltex), have confirmed there are currently no signs that Thailand will run

items.title: Motorcycle taxi rider suspected of public indecency in Songkhla
items.desc: Motorcycle taxi rider suspected of public indecency in Songkhla Legacy Police in Songkhla’s Sadao district have launched a search for a suspect after two women reported disturbing encounters with a man accused of performing indecent acts in public . The incident was reported today, March 15, by 32 year old Nurhayati Salaemae, a resident of Samnak Kham subdistrict in Sadao district. According to the complaint, the suspect was described as a tall, thin man with dark skin who wore a black face mask and rode a motorcycle while following the victim from the plant quarantine checkpoint area to Ban P

items.title: Transgender suspect charged after robbing Japanese man in Pattaya
items.desc: Transgender suspect charged after robbing Japanese man in Pattaya Legacy A transgender massage worker surrendered to police in Pattaya yesterday, March 14, after a Japanese tourist reported being assaulted and robbed inside a hotel room earlier this week. The suspect, identified as A, arrived at Pattaya City Police Station and returned 9,000 baht allegedly taken from the victim’s hotel room. A deputy investigation officer received the money on behalf of the victim. Photo via DailyNews The case stems from an incident on March 13 at a hotel in Nong Prue, Bang Lamung district, Chon Buri. items.title: Foreign woman seriously injured after fall from Pattaya hotel
items.desc: Foreign woman seriously injured after fall from Pattaya hotel Legacy A hotel security guard in Pattaya discovered a seriously injured foreign woman lying in bushes beside a hotel building early this morning, March 15, after hearing a loud crash outside the property. Rescue workers from the Sawang Boriboon Thammasathan Pattaya Rescue Centre and officers from Pattaya City Police Station responded to the incident at a hotel in Nong Prue, Bang Lamung district, after receiving the report shortly after 4.06am. Photo via Siam Chon News Upon arrival, they found the woman, believed to be a foreign nati

items.title: Two Thai women filmed brawling on Phuket street over customers
items.desc: Two Thai women filmed brawling on Phuket street over customers Legacy A late-night fight between two Thai women , reportedly over customers, in Soi Bangla, Patong, Phuket , today, March 15, was captured on video and later shared online , drawing criticisms on social media. The incident occurred around midnight in the busy nightlife street in Patong subdistrict, Kathu district, an area frequented by both Thai and foreign visitors. The footage shows the two women engaged in a heated verbal argument before the confrontation escalated into a physical fight in the middle of the street. As the alter

items.title: German tourist dies in bike crash at Phayao’s Triple Heaven Spot
items.desc: German tourist dies in bike crash at Phayao’s Triple Heaven Spot Legacy A German tourist tragically lost his life while riding a big bike in Phayao with friends, after crashing into a cliff on March 14. Police from Chiang Muan district in Phayao province were informed of a big bike accident on the Phayao-Chiang Muan route, at a location known as the Triple Heaven Spot in Chiang Muan district. The accident resulted in a fatality, prompting coordination with forensic doctors from Chiang Muan Hospital and rescue workers from the Siam Ruamjai Pu In Foundation in Chiang Muan to investigate. U

items.title: Cambodian singer calls Lisa the only ‘Siem girl’ he would date
items.desc: Cambodian singer calls Lisa the only ‘Siem girl’ he would date Legacy A Cambodian boyband member has come under fire in Thailand after comments in a recent interview about his ideal partner referenced Thai women and global pop star Lisa Manobal . The remarks were made by 23 year old Cambodian singer Erien, a member of the boyband ENO STRONG MAN, during a recent interview where he discussed his preferences in relationships.
